description: Catalog of AI tools that THIRD PARTIES use on you — your employer, doctor, HR, recruiter, interviewer. You're the data, not the user. This catalog documents what each tool does, who controls it, and what legal rights you have to object.

contexts.workplace: Tools your employer/colleagues may deploy on meetings, chat, email
contexts.hr-recruiting: Tools HR / recruiters use to analyze applications, interviews, performance
contexts.medical: Tools healthcare providers use on patient encounters
contexts.customer-service: Tools companies use on YOUR support calls

jurisdictions.EU: GDPR Article 22 + Article 21 (object to automated decision-making + profiling)
jurisdictions.US-federal: Limited — FTC guidance + ADA for AI in hiring
jurisdictions.US-state-IL: Illinois AI Video Interview Act (ADVID) — must consent to AI in interviews
jurisdictions.US-state-CA: CCPA + AB-331 (automated decision tools in employment)
jurisdictions.US-state-NY: NYC Local Law 144 — bias audit requirement for AEDTs
jurisdictions.HIPAA: US Federal — covered AI must be a Business Associate + HIPAA-compliant

zoom-ai-companion.whoDeploys: Any meeting host (requires their plan, applies to all participants)
zoom-ai-companion.notification: Zoom shows a banner when AI is active — but many users miss it or meetings are scheduled before you join
zoom-ai-companion.defaultBehavior: Host can enable without asking participants; participants see a notification
zoom-ai-companion.optOut: Can LEAVE the meeting. Cannot opt out while staying. Must be explicit to host.
zoom-ai-companion.notes: Zoom clarified 2023 that customer content is NOT used for third-party model training. AI Companion summaries stay within the customer account. Still: meeting transcripts are stored + searchable.

otter-ai.whoDeploys: Auto-joining bot — ANY Otter user can add it to a calendar invite
otter-ai.notification: Bot joins meeting as 'OtterPilot' — shows as participant
otter-ai.defaultBehavior: Training is opt-in by default (good); transcripts shared with inviter's team by default
otter-ai.optOut: Must ASK to remove bot. If you refuse recording, inviter must disable or everyone must leave.
otter-ai.notes: Otter also has `vanish ai-opt-out --otter` for your own Otter account's training toggle. This entry is for meetings OTHERS have Otter in.

fireflies-ai.notification: Bot appears as 'Fred' or similar named participant
fireflies-ai.defaultBehavior: Records by default; participants see bot but may not realize it's AI
fireflies-ai.optOut: Must ask host to kick the bot or decline meeting
fireflies-ai.notes: Fireflies has enterprise + free tiers. Sales-focused: transcripts get scored for 'buying signals' etc.

fathom.whoDeploys: Zoom extension — any Zoom user with Fathom can enable
fathom.notification: Zoom shows recording indicator; Fathom logo in the bot
fathom.defaultBehavior: Records when host enables; transcripts become searchable library
fathom.optOut: Leave the meeting or ask host to disable
fathom.notes: Fathom pitches 'free forever' tier to individuals — common in startups.

gong.whoDeploys: Enterprise sales orgs — typically auto-records ALL external-facing calls
gong.notification: Disclosure varies by company — many sales reps don't disclose unless asked
gong.defaultBehavior: Company-wide recording policy; employees often don't know what's captured
gong.optOut: Strongest when you're the CUSTOMER side — you can refuse and the sales rep must accommodate
gong.notes: If you're a buyer/customer on a sales call: the sales org is likely recording via Gong/Chorus. You have the right to refuse per your jurisdiction (2-party consent states in US). In EU under GDPR, silence is NOT consent.

chorus-zoominfo.defaultBehavior: Similar to Gong but with ZoomInfo data integration (pulls public data on your company)
chorus-zoominfo.optOut: As customer: refuse recording. As employee: company policy usually mandatory.

read-ai.whoDeploys: Auto-joining meeting bot; also Zoom/Gmail extensions
read-ai.notification: Bot joins named 'Read'; sends summary emails after
read-ai.defaultBehavior: Records by default; analyzes 'engagement' including facial expressions — more invasive than audio-only tools
read-ai.optOut: Ask host to remove. Disable camera to limit facial-analysis input.
read-ai.notes: Read.ai's facial-expression scoring is particularly controversial — implies scoring your 'engagement' without consent.

microsoft-teams-copilot.whoDeploys: Enterprise admins; per-meeting host can toggle
microsoft-teams-copilot.defaultBehavior: Enterprise-controlled — typically disabled by default in E3/E5 licenses; admins roll out selectively
microsoft-teams-copilot.optOut: Leave meeting. Can request admin-level exclusion but implementation varies.
microsoft-teams-copilot.notes: M365 Copilot enterprise data protection means the content is NOT used for model training — but IS stored within your org's tenant + admin-readable.

hirevue.notification: Candidate sees a recording consent screen
hirevue.defaultBehavior: Mandatory participation for roles using it — refusing may exclude you from the job
hirevue.optOut: Request live human interview instead (explicit accommodation request). Some jurisdictions require it.
hirevue.notes: HireVue discontinued face-analysis in 2021 after civil-rights criticism — still uses voice + response content. IL requires disclosure (AI Video Interview Act). NYC Local Law 144 requires annual bias audits.

pymetrics-harver.notification: Candidate completes 'games' — AI inference not usually disclosed
pymetrics-harver.optOut: Request alternative assessment (accommodation). Jurisdictions with ADA/disability law may require.
pymetrics-harver.notes: Pymetrics's personality-from-games approach has been criticized as pseudo-scientific. Candidates can request human-only evaluation.

abridge.notification: Patient usually informed verbally; consent forms vary by state
abridge.defaultBehavior: Many providers enable by default; patient must explicitly object
abridge.optOut: Right to decline under HIPAA + state law. Provider must accommodate human-only note-taking.
abridge.notes: Abridge is HIPAA-compliant Business Associate. Still: the conversation is analyzed by AI, audio may be retained for model improvement (check their BAA).

nuance-dax.whoDeploys: Large health systems (Mayo, Northwestern, Providence, etc.)
nuance-dax.notification: Varies by health system; often buried in intake paperwork
nuance-dax.optOut: Explicit request. Providers must offer alternative. HIPAA complaint possible if denied.
nuance-dax.notes: DAX captures AMBIENT audio during your visit — your voice, the doctor's, and anyone else in the room.

suki-ai.defaultBehavior: Opt-in typically required but implementation inconsistent

workplace-meeting.template:
To: [meeting organizer]

Subject: Request to disable AI recording/analysis in our meeting(s)

Hi [name],

I'm writing to formally request that AI-based transcription, recording, or analysis tools be disabled during meetings I attend. This includes tools such as {{toolNames}}.

My reasons:
1. I have not consented to my voice, likeness, or communication content being processed by third-party AI systems beyond the meeting itself.
2. {{jurisdictionClause}}
3. AI summaries risk misrepresenting my contributions — I prefer direct communication on the record.

I'm happy to participate in meetings that do NOT use AI tools. For meetings where the tool is enabled, please let me know in advance so I can decide whether to attend.

Thank you,
[Your name]

sales-call-recording.template:
To: [sales contact]

Subject: Recording consent for our upcoming call

Hi [name],

Before our call on [date], please confirm whether your team uses call-recording or AI-analysis tools (e.g., Gong, Chorus, ZoomInfo, Fathom) on external-facing sales conversations.

If so, I do NOT consent to recording or AI processing of my participation. {{jurisdictionClause}}

If recording is required by your company policy, please arrange for a call where it's disabled — otherwise I'll need to decline.

Thank you,
[Your name]

ai-interview.template:
To: [recruiter / hiring manager]

Subject: Request for human-conducted interview (not AI-mediated)

Hi [name],

I understand the {{companyName}} interview process includes an AI-assisted step using {{toolNames}}. I'm writing to formally request an alternative: a live interview conducted by human interviewers only.

{{jurisdictionClause}}

I'm a strong candidate and would appreciate being evaluated by people rather than algorithmic inference. Please let me know if an alternative path is available.

Thank you,
[Your name]

medical-ai.template:
To: [provider / clinic]

Subject: Request to decline AI transcription during my visits

Hi [provider/clinic],

I'm writing to decline use of {{toolNames}} (or any similar AI-based recording/transcription tool) during my medical visits.

Under HIPAA and my rights as a patient, I prefer that my conversations with my healthcare provider NOT be processed by AI tools, including but not limited to:
- Audio recording or transcription
- Retention of my voice or clinical conversations
- Third-party AI analysis, even under a BAA

{{jurisdictionClause}}

Please note this preference in my patient record and ensure it's honored across all my visits. I am happy to discuss alternative documentation methods (manual note-taking) with my provider.

Thank you,
[Your name]
